page: Microsoft Azure D32ps_v5 is an ARM-based virtual machine belonging to the DPSv5 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs powered by an Ampere Altra ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor operating at 3.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Cache specifications consist of 64 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark evaluations place the D32ps_v5 mostly in the average performance tier. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance score is 1822.98, while the multi-core score reaches 57217.14. In Geekbench testing, the single-core score of 1104.0 falls into the bottom 25% tier, whereas the multi-core score of 12952.0 sits in the average tier. Memory bandwidth tests demonstrate a read-only small block cached speed of 2231.53 GB/sec, alongside large block uncached read and write speeds of 170.09 GB/sec and 77.1 GB/sec, respectively.

The instance shows average capabilities across various workloads, including database operations, web serving, and software compilation. Passmark database operations score is 20048.29, and Redis SET operations achieve 41.25 million operations per second. Static web serving throughput is measured at 32.88 GB/sec. For language model inference, the server handles small 135M models at 212.53 text generation tokens per second, but performance drops to a poor tier of 2.31 tokens per second for large 70B models. This indicates a clear tradeoff where the ARM architecture is suited for general-purpose scale-out workloads, web servers, and application servers, but is less effective for heavy single-threaded tasks or large-scale AI inference.
description: The Microsoft Azure D32ps_v5 is an ARM-based virtual machine from the DPSv5 family, utilizing a 32-core Ampere Altra Neoverse-N1 processor clocked at 3.0 GHz. It features 128.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio, with no local storage or GPUs. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance delivers average multi-core performance, as shown by a Geekbench multi-core score of 12952.0 and a stress-ng multi-core score of 57217.14. Single-core performance is a notable tradeoff, with Geekbench single-core results falling into the bottom 25% tier. The server is suited for general-purpose workloads, including static web serving, software compilation, and database operations, but exhibits poor performance on large-scale LLM inference tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D32ps_v5 is an ARM-based virtual machine featuring 32 dedicated Ampere Neoverse-N1 cores, 128 GB of RAM, and average multi-core performance suited for general-purpose workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D32ps_v5 is a 32-core ARM-based virtual machine with 128 GB of RAM, designed for general-purpose and web serving workloads.
tagline: A dedicated 32-core ARMv8 virtual machine offering balanced memory allocation and average multi-core performance for general-purpose scale-out workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated Ampere Altra Neoverse-N1 ARMv8 cores running at 3.0 GHz
128.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core
Average multi-core performance for web serving and database operations
No local storage or integrated GPU accelerators included
Tradeoff of weaker single-core performance and poor large-scale LLM inference
